description_long: This is a retro-style portable radio receiver with a rectangular body, featuring a sturdy, metallic handle arching over the top for easy carrying. The front face displays a large, circular black speaker grille on the right, and a blue-tinted cassette compartment with a transparent window on the left, flanked by a set of tactile control buttons and two round knobs on the top. The sides are relatively plain, with one side showing a cylindrical tuning knob and the other side mostly flat. The back panel is blue with ventilation slits and a small access panel, likely for batteries or servicing. The color palette consists of orange-brown, blue, black, and metallic gray, giving it a distinct vintage urban aesthetic. The overall construction appears to be primarily plastic with some metal components for durability and function.
description: A vintage portable radio receiver with a metallic handle, blue and orange body, large speaker grille, and cassette compartment, featuring tactile buttons and knobs.
description_view_0: Front view shows the speaker grille, cassette compartment, control buttons, and knobs prominently.
description_view_1: Left side view highlights the cylindrical tuning knob and the thickness of the handle.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals the blue back panel with ventilation slits and a small access panel.
description_view_3: Right side view is mostly flat, showing the edge of the cassette compartment and the handle.
